2026-04-06T19:50:24.5329907Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-06T19:50:24.5330740Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-06T19:50:24.5331180Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-06T19:50:24.5331551Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-06T19:50:24.5331854Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-06T19:50:24.5332087Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-06T19:50:24.5332243Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-06T19:50:24.5332419Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-06T19:50:24.5332585Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-06T19:50:24.5332809Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-06T19:50:24.5333009Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-06T19:50:24.5333293Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-06T19:50:24.5333549Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-06T19:52:23.5829570Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-06T19:50:59.998546156Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57822,\"title\":\"[Identity] Docs: ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md snippets out of sync with .NET 9 API updates\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Identity`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57522) by `@fowl2`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57522 enhanced `ClientCertificateCredential` documentation with platform certificate store support. In `CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs`, two snippets were updated to use the recommended .NET 9+ API (`X509CertificateLoader`) via `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` conditional blocks:\\n\\n- `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` — now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadPkcs12FromFile` on .NET 9+\\n- `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` — now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadCertificateFromFile` on .NET 9+\\n\\nHowever, `Update-Snippets.ps1` was not run after these test file changes, so `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` still shows the old `.NET 8` form without the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` conditional branches.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` snippet `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` shows `new X509Certificate2(...)` but the backing test code now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadPkcs12FromFile` on .NET 9+\\n- `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` snippet `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` shows `new X509Certificate2(_path)` but the backing test code now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadCertificateFromFile` on .NET 9+\\n- Users on .NET 9+ are directed to a deprecated API (`X509Certificate2` constructor with path) rather than the recommended `X509CertificateLoader` API\\n- The `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateFromStore` snippet references `CertificateNotFoundException` without any explanation that this is a user-defined exception type — readers may be confused about where it comes from\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\nNo files need to be manually edited. The snippet content is generated automatically from `CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs` via `Update-Snippets.ps1`. Simply run that script to sync the markdown.\\n\\nHowever, the `CreateFromStore` snippet gap requires a manual prose change in the markdown.\\n\\n**File to edit:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/samples/ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md`\\n\\nIn the \\u0026#34;Loading certificates from an X509Store\\u0026#34; section, after the `CreateFromStore` snippet block, add a note explaining `CertificateNotFoundException`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** `CertificateNotFoundException` in the example above is a user-defined exception. You can replace it with any appropriate exception type (such as `InvalidOperationException`) or define your own:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```csharp\\n\\u0026gt; public sealed class CertificateNotFoundException(string message) : Exception(message) { }\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippet content for `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` and `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` must be regenerated from the test source file. The test file already contains the correct `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` branches — just run the snippet update tooling.\\n\\n**Test file:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/tests/samples/CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs`\\n\\nThe relevant snippets already contain the correct code with `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` / `#else` / `#endif` blocks.\\n\\n**README/Samples location:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/samples/ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md`\\n\\nAfter running `Update-Snippets.ps1`, verify that the following snippet placeholders in the markdown were updated to include the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` block:\\n\\n- `` ```C# Snippet:Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert ``\\n- `` ```C# Snippet:Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential ``\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` is a standalone samples doc (not the main README). Its current structure covers the required scenarios. No structural changes are needed beyond the snippet sync and `CertificateNotFoundException` clarification.\\n\\nThe main `README.md` at `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` already has all required sections. No changes needed there.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` — regenerates snippet blocks in markdown from `#region Snippet:` tags in test files. After running, check the git diff on `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` to confirm the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` blocks now appear in the snippet output\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24047751321/agentic_workflow) · ● 486.6K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57822\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-06T19:48:10Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-06T19:48:10Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

In `CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs`, two snippets were updated to use the recommended .NET 9+ API (`X509CertificateLoader`) via `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` conditional blocks:\\n\\n- `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` — now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadPkcs12FromFile` on .NET 9+\\n- `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` — now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadCertificateFromFile` on .NET 9+\\n\\nHowever, `Update-Snippets.ps1` was not run after these test file changes, so `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` still shows the old `.NET 8` form without the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` conditional branches.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` snippet `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` shows `new X509Certificate2(...)` but the backing test code now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadPkcs12FromFile` on .NET 9+\\n- `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` snippet `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` shows `new X509Certificate2(_path)` but the backing test code now uses `X509CertificateLoader.LoadCertificateFromFile` on .NET 9+\\n- Users on .NET 9+ are directed to a deprecated API (`X509Certificate2` constructor with path) rather than the recommended `X509CertificateLoader` API\\n- The `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateFromStore` snippet references `CertificateNotFoundException` without any explanation that this is a user-defined exception type — readers may be confused about where it comes from\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\nNo files need to be manually edited. Simply run that script to sync the markdown.\\n\\nHowever, the `CreateFromStore` snippet gap requires a manual prose change in the markdown.\\n\\n**File to edit:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/samples/ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md`\\n\\nIn the \\u0026#34;Loading certificates from an X509Store\\u0026#34; section, after the `CreateFromStore` snippet block, add a note explaining `CertificateNotFoundException`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** `CertificateNotFoundException` in the example above is a user-defined exception. You can replace it with any appropriate exception type (such as `InvalidOperationException`) or define your own:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```csharp\\n\\u0026gt; public sealed class CertificateNotFoundException(string message) : Exception(message) { }\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippet content for `Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert` and `Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential` must be regenerated from the test source file. The test file already contains the correct `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` branches — just run the snippet update tooling.\\n\\n**Test file:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/tests/samples/CertificateCredentialSnippets.cs`\\n\\nThe relevant snippets already contain the correct code with `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` / `#else` / `#endif` blocks.\\n\\n**README/Samples location:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/samples/ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md`\\n\\nAfter running `Update-Snippets.ps1`, verify that the following snippet placeholders in the markdown were updated to include the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` block:\\n\\n- `` ```C# Snippet:Identity_CertificateCredential_CreateWithX509Cert ``\\n- `` ```C# Snippet:Identity_CertificateCredential_RotatingCredential ``\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` is a standalone samples doc (not the main README). Its current structure covers the required scenarios. No structural changes are needed beyond the snippet sync and `CertificateNotFoundException` clarification.\\n\\nThe main `README.md` at `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` already has all required sections. No changes needed there.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` — regenerates snippet blocks in markdown from `#region Snippet:` tags in test files. After running, check the git diff on `ClientCertificateCredentialSamples.md` to confirm the `#if NET9_0_OR_GREATER` blocks now appear in the snippet output\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24047751321/agentic_workflow) · ● 486.6K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57822\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-06T19:48:10Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-06T19:48:10Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
